item.title: Iron Ore Falls to Two-Week Low on China Steel Demand Concerns
item.description: Iron ore fell for a fourth session in Singapore, the longest run of losses in almost three months, on concerns about a worsening demand outlook in China.

item.title: S. Korea Household Lending Accelerates Amid Seoul Housing Rally
item.description: South Korea’s household loan growth accelerated in the first quarter, highlighting persistent housing-related financial risks facing the Bank of Korea ahead of its first rate-setting meeting under the new governor next week.

item.title: Metals Slide as US-Iran Situation Makes Outlook ‘Very Binary’
item.description: Copper fell as investors continued to fret about the global economy’s prospects, offering a lukewarm response to US President Donald Trump’s hints at progress toward a peace deal with Iran.

item.title: India Raises Fuel Prices Again as Iran War Squeezes Refiners
item.description: India’s state-run refiners raised fuel prices for the second time in less than a week, as they grapple with economic losses after the Middle East conflict drove the cost of crude significantly higher.

item.title: Bond Yields Gain Edge Over Nifty Dividends, Challenging Stocks
item.description: Patchy profits, foreign outflows and limited exposure to AI are hurting Indian equities.

item.title: Australia Urgently ‘Needs a Lot More Gas,’ Energy Executives Say
item.description: Australia must invest in producing more natural gas to avoid a domestic shortfall later this decade, fossil fuel executives said.

item.title: China’s Aluminum Output Surge Tests Capacity and Demand Limits
item.description: Chinese aluminum smelters are running hot — and starting to test both capacity constraints and limitations on demand.

item.title: RBA Says Hike Gave Space to Assess War Impact on Households
item.description: Australia’s central bank said raising interest rates for a third consecutive meeting would give the board scope to monitor how households and firms respond to the impact of the conflict in the Middle East that has sent fuel prices surging.

item.title: JX Metals Prices $1.6 Billion of Convertibles at Top of Range
item.description: JX Advanced Metals Corp. has priced its zero-coupon convertible bonds at the top of the marketed range, underscoring solid investor demand for equity-linked securities in Japan.

item.title: Hedge Fund Beats Peers With Bets on Oil Tankers While Cutting AI
item.description: A Hong Kong hedge fund that outperformed peers says shipping stocks are a better trade than artificial intelligence, given the risks of tech companies overspending.

item.title: Analog Devices Is Said to Near $1.5 Billion Deal for Empower
item.description: Analog Devices Inc. is in advanced talks to acquire closely held Empower Semiconductor for $1.5 billion in cash, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: New Zealand to Cut Public Service Jobs in Savings Push
item.description: New Zealand expects significant savings from an overhaul of its public service, which will eliminate about 8,700 jobs, merge some government departments and extend the use of AI.

item.title: Korea’s World-Beating Market Rally Hinges on a Handful of Stocks
item.description: Supercharged gains in a handful of companies and weak market breadth are stoking concerns that the world’s hottest stock market is overheating in some pockets.

item.title: Google, Blackstone to Create Cloud Business to Handle AI Demand
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google is set to create an artificial intelligence cloud business with Blackstone Inc., aiming to compete with companies like CoreWeave Inc. in a burgeoning market, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Bond Yield Surge Poses Threat to Asia’s AI-Fueled Stock Rally
item.description: Rising US bond yields pose a threat to Asia’s stock rally, as growing inflation fears offset ongoing optimism over benefits from the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Australia Treasurer Defends Contentious Investment Tax Changes
item.description: Australia’s Treasurer Jim Chalmers has defended the proposed tax changes in the government’s recently announced budget, arguing that they increase fairness for investors and will still tax capital gains at rates lower than other overseas markets.

item.title: Gray Power Market Thrives as Iran War Leaves Pakistan in the Dark
item.description: A historic energy crunch is pushing ordinary citizens to find their own fixes as temperatures rise.

item.title: Japan’s Faster Economic Growth Supports BOJ’s Case for Hike
item.description: Japan’s economy grew much faster than expected at the start of the year, supporting the case for further Bank of Japan interest-rate hikes, though the outlook remains highly uncertain due to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: RBA Warns Risk ‘Elevated’ of Price Expectations Drifting Higher
item.description: The risk of inflation expectations in Australia drifting higher is “elevated,” a senior Reserve Bank official said, warning that if they were to become untethered a sharp economic slowdown may be needed to lower them.

item.title: Meta Moves 7,000 Workers Into AI Roles Ahead of Job Cuts
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is reassigning 7,000 workers to new jobs related to artificial intelligence, according to an internal memo, part of a broad corporate restructuring that includes planned staff reductions later this week.

item.title: East Coast Power Prices Surge as Heat Spurs Emergency Grid Alert
item.description: Power prices are surging in the US East after the largest US grid operator declared an emergency as soaring temperatures descend on the region.

item.title: Gold Extends Gain as Hopes for Iran Truce Ease Inflation Fears
item.description: Gold extended a gain as the prospect of a US-Iran ceasefire deal eased some inflationary concerns that have weighed on bullion.

item.title: Oil Slips as Trump Spurs Optimism Over Iran Deal: Markets Wrap
item.description: Oil dropped in early Asia trading after President Donald Trump said he was holding off on fresh military strikes, boosting hopes for a deal that could end the war and revive energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: New Zealand to Cut Public Service Jobs, Merge Some Departments in Overhaul
item.description: New Zealand will overhaul its public service, including job cuts and mergers between some government departments, as it looks to trim costs, Finance Minister Nicola Willis said.

item.title: Oil Declines After Trump Says He Called Off Strike on Iran
item.description: Oil fell after President Donald Trump said he’d called off a strike on Iran planned for Tuesday following an appeal by the leaders of Persian Gulf allies.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Group to Sell Chicago Parking Meters to Stonepeak
item.description: A Morgan Stanley-led group has reached a deal to sell its lease of Chicago’s parking meters to New York-based Stonepeak, Mayor Brandon Johnson’s office said on Monday.

item.title: From Medical Tourism to Casinos, Oil Shock Hits Southeast Asia
item.description: A growing list of companies in Southeast Asia are sounding the warning bell over rising energy costs and weakening consumer demand as the conflict in the Middle East drags into its third month.

item.title: AMP Trims ‘Frothy’ Private Credit Assets in Investment Pivot
item.description: Australian pension and wealth manager AMP Ltd. has been scaling back its exposure to private credit, citing an increasingly frothy market as its shifts money toward assets such as infrastructure.

item.title: Japan Official Sows Doubt Over Potential US Treasuries Sales
item.description: A senior Japanese Finance Ministry official sowed doubt over any prospects for the nation to sell US Treasuries as part of any move to prop up the yen, suggesting the maneuver could end up being counterproductive.

item.title: AI Chip Startup Tenstorrent Draws Takeover Interest From Intel, Qualcomm
item.description: Artificial intelligence chip startup Tenstorrent Inc. is drawing early takeover interest from prospective buyers at a moment of renewed momentum for upstarts looking to challenge Nvidia Corp. and Advanced Micro Devices Inc.
